====ARABIC START====
You are generating the lexicon for a synthetic language.

The language uses the following orthography rules:

=== ORTHOGRAPHY START ===
Orthography rules (global):
- All words are lowercase letters a–z only.
- No punctuation, digits, diacritics, or other symbols.
- No capital letters.

Word shape:
- Content words: at least 4 letters.
- Function words (particles, case markers, etc.): 1–3 letters.

Anti-English:
- Do NOT use real English words (e.g. "the", "and", "is", "in", "to").
- Avoid common English morphemes: "ing", "ed", "tion", "sion", "able",
  "less", "ful", "pre", "re", "un", "dis".
- If a word looks close to an English word, change at least 2 letters.
• Language-specific orthography:
- Letter inventory: {a, i, u, b, d, f, g, h, j, k, l, m, n, q, r, s, t, w, y, z}.
- Use default CV/CVC syllables; do not spell long vowels as double letters.

=== ORTHOGRAPHY END ===

It has the following typological profile:

=== FEATURE VECTOR START ===
{
  "morphological_fusion": "fusional",
  "affixation_balance": "suffixing",
  "gender_inventory": "few",
  "number_marking": "obligatory",
  "case_inventory": "moderate",
  "numeral_classifiers": "non-classifier",
  "basic_word_order": "VSO",
  "adposition_type": "prepositional",
  "genitive_noun_order": "genitive-after-noun",
  "adjective_noun_order": "adjective-after-noun",
  "relative_clause_order": "head-initial",
  "relative_clause_marking": "relative-pronoun",
  "alignment_typology": "nominative–accusative",
  "agreement_pattern": "subject-only",
  "tense_system": "binary-past-nonpast",
  "aspect_system": "moderate",
  "mood_system": "moderate",
  "negation_strategy": "verbal-affix",
  "causative_morphology": "present",
  "passive_morphology": "present",
  "applicative_morphology": "absent",
  "question_marking_strategy": "wh-fronting"
}
=== FEATURE VECTOR END ===

Its affix inventory is:

=== AFFIXES START ===
{
  "inflectional": {
    "number": [
      {
        "name": "PL",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "rimu",
        "function": "plural (more than one referent; singular is unmarked)"
      }
    ],
    "tense_aspect_mood": [
      {
        "name": "PST",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "duq",
        "function": "past (binary contrast with unmarked nonpast)"
      },
      {
        "name": "IPFV",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "galy",
        "function": "imperfective (ongoing or habitual viewpoint, compatible with either tense)"
      },
      {
        "name": "PFV",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "suq",
        "function": "perfective (completed, whole-event viewpoint, compatible with either tense)"
      },
      {
        "name": "IRR",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "tajm",
        "function": "irrealis / modal (desired, possible, counterfactual or non-actual situations)"
      },
      {
        "name": "NEG",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "huz",
        "function": "verbal negation (clausal negator attaching to the main verb)"
      }
    ],
    "voice_valence": [
      {
        "name": "PASS",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "zulam",
        "function": "passive (demotes or omits agent, promotes patient to subject)"
      },
      {
        "name": "CAUS",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "tibar",
        "function": "causative (adds a higher agent/causer, demotes original subject)"
      }
    ]
  },
  "derivational": [
    {
      "name": "AGENT_N",
      "type": "suffix",
      "form": "namur",
      "from_pos": "verb",
      "to_pos": "noun",
      "function": "agentive (one who habitually or characteristically does X)"
    },
    {
      "name": "ACTION_N",
      "type": "suffix",
      "form": "duris",
      "from_pos": "verb",
      "to_pos": "noun",
      "function": "eventive/action noun (the act or process of doing X)"
    },
    {
      "name": "RESULT_N",
      "type": "suffix",
      "form": "galm",
      "from_pos": "verb",
      "to_pos": "noun",
      "function": "result noun (product or outcome of doing X)"
    },
    {
      "name": "ABSTR_N",
      "type": "suffix",
      "form": "siqur",
      "from_pos": "adjective",
      "to_pos": "noun",
      "function": "abstract quality noun (the property or quality of being X)"
    },
    {
      "name": "ADJ_FROM_N",
      "type": "suffix",
      "form": "rilug",
      "from_pos": "noun",
      "to_pos": "adjective",
      "function": "relational/adjectival (having, relating to, or characterized by X)"
    },
    {
      "name": "ADJ_FROM_V",
      "type": "suffix",
      "form": "huqi",
      "from_pos": "verb",
      "to_pos": "adjective",
      "function": "verbal adjective (inclined or apt to do X; often used for participant roles)"
    }
  ]
}
=== AFFIXES END ===

Your task:
Create a vocabulary list of standalone words for this language.

GLOBAL RULES:

1. Lexicon entries are standalone words
   - Every entry must be a full word that can appear by itself in a sentence.
   - Do NOT include bound morphemes (affixes).
   - Do NOT include partial stems or segmented forms.
   - Do NOT use hyphens or any explicit morpheme separators in the word form.

2. Orthography
   - All forms must be lowercase.
   - All forms must obey the orthography rules exactly.
   - No digits, punctuation, or uppercase letters.
   - No form may obviously resemble an English or real-world word.

3. No inflected forms
   - Lexicon entries must be base forms only (citation forms).
   - DO NOT include any inflected forms (e.g., plural, case-marked, tense-marked, aspect-marked, voice-marked, etc.).
   - Even if an inflectional affix exists in the inventory, do NOT bake it into the lexicon form.
   - The lexicon represents dictionary-style base words, not paradigm members.

4. Relationship to affixes
   - Do NOT list any affix forms (inflectional or derivational) as lexicon entries.
   - If the affix inventory includes derivational morphology (e.g., agentive, diminutive),
     you may include words whose meanings correspond to those categories, but:
       • list them as full word forms, and
       • do NOT segment or expose the affix inside the word form.
   - For each derivational affix in the inventory, include at least one lexical item whose meaning
     reflects that derivational function (e.g., an agentive noun, a diminutive noun, etc.),
     but NEVER include the affix itself as a standalone form.

5. Coverage
   Include at least:
   - Basic nouns (persons, objects, locations, abstract concepts).
   - Basic verbs (common actions, processes, states).
   - Basic adjectives (qualities, states).
   - Function words needed by the typology:
       • pronouns,
       • adpositions (prepositions/postpositions, depending on the feature vector),
       • basic question words,
       • demonstratives or determiners if appropriate.

OUTPUT FORMAT (CSV):

Output a CSV with EXACTLY this header:

form,pos,translation

Each row must contain exactly 3 comma-separated fields:
- form: the full conlang word form (lemma, uninflected).
- pos: part of speech (e.g., noun, verb, adj, adposition, pronoun).
- translation: the English meaning.

No commas are allowed inside any field.

Output ONLY the CSV. No commentary or explanation.
====ARABIC END====
